name: Almonnaise
recipeInstructions:
1. Water can be substituted for soy milk.
2. Process almonds to a fine powder in a blender or food processor.
3. Add soy milk, nutritional yeast, garlic powder, and salt to the powdered almonds. Blend well.
4. Gradually add remaining soy milk while blending until the mixture forms a smooth cream.
5. With the blender running on low, remove the lid and drizzle in oil in thin streams until the desired consistency is reached.
6. Continue blending for 1 minute longer to allow the mixture to thicken.
7. Transfer the almonnaise to a tightly sealed container and refrigerate. It will keep for 10-14 days in the refrigerator.
8. Note: Do not be discouraged if, on occasion, your almonnaise does not thicken as expected. Homemade mayonnaise is quite sensitive to preparation, and sometimes it just doesn't respond. This recipe has been successfully made by many people, but occasionally it fails even for the author of The American Vegetarian Cookbook.
